actions.heroSubtitle: Join the actions that matter to you. Every signature counts.
actions.goFurtherSubtitle: Become a stakeholder in legal actions by choosing your plan.

tarifs.heroText: Wejustice receives no subsidies, no public funding, no private support. Our total independence is the price of our freedom to act. We rely solely on the support of our members. It is the only way for the 5<sup>th</sup> power to exist and remain accessible to all.
tarifs.choosePlanSubtitle: Your subscription funds the legal proceedings, lawyers, and tools that make Justice accessible.
tarifs.reducedRateInfo: Reserved for low-income individuals (students, unemployed, low pensions) who agree to provide supporting documents upon request.
tarifs.joinFreeSubtitle: Create your free account to sign unlimited petitions.
tarifs.orgSubtitle: Unions, companies, associations and NGOs: Wejustice allows you to launch collective legal actions to defend your members and your cause.
tarifs.upTo2PersonsDesc: For me and one family member
tarifs.upTo3PersonsDesc: For me and two family members
tarifs.faq1q: What is the difference between signing and joining?
tarifs.faq1a: Signing an action is free and unlimited: it expresses your support. Joining means becoming a stakeholder in the legal action, which is governed by your plan.
tarifs.faq2q: Can I change my plan along the way?
tarifs.faq2a: Yes, you can upgrade to a higher plan at any time. The difference will be calculated pro rata based on your remaining commitment.
tarifs.faq3a: The reduced rate (-50%) is reserved for people in financial hardship (unemployed, students, low pensions). Proof may be requested.
tarifs.faq4q: How does the group plan work?
tarifs.faq4a: With the Maxi (2 people) or Aura (3 people) plans, you fund the subscription for your loved ones. Each person has their own account and their own actions.
tarifs.faq5a: Wejustice is in the process of obtaining public interest status. We will inform you as soon as the tax deductibility is active.
tarifs.faq6q: What happens if I don't renew?
tarifs.faq6a: Your signatures remain active. You simply won't be able to join new actions until you reactivate a plan.

blog.title: Blog
blog.heroSubtitle: Analysis, investigations, insights. Follow our legal battles and collective justice news.
blog.takeActionDesc: Join collective actions that advance justice.

tribune.heroDesc: Report injustices. Build a structured citizen case with facts, evidence and demands. Submit it to the community's judgment.
tribune.notifySuccess: You will be notified when the People's Tribune launches.
tribune.howItWorksDesc: Build a strong citizen case and submit it to the community's vote.
tribune.step1Desc: Describe the injustice with dated and verifiable facts. Identify the target: organization, institution or systemic practice.
tribune.step2Desc: Documents, photos, videos, links. Up to 10 MB per file. Evidence strengthens the credibility of your case with the community.
tribune.step3Desc: Your case is published after moderation. Citizens vote: Support, Opposition or Abstention. The targeted organization can exercise its right of reply.
tribune.dossierStep1Desc: Who or what is responsible for the injustice? Organization, institution or systemic practice.
tribune.dossierStep2Desc: List dated and verifiable events in chronological order. Each fact must be factual.
tribune.dossierStep3Desc: PDF documents, photos, videos, links. Up to 10 MB per file. Optional but strengthens your case.
tribune.dossierStep4Desc: Financial compensation, public apology, policy change, transparency. Be specific.
tribune.dossierStep5Desc: Your case is reviewed by the team. If valid, it is published and submitted to the community's vote.
tribune.forWhomDesc: The People's Tribune is open to everyone who wants to make their voice heard.
tribune.citizensDesc: Report an injustice you experienced. Build a solid case and let the community judge.
tribune.scientistsDesc: Publish cases supported by scientific evidence to enlighten public debate.
tribune.journalistsDesc: Document matters of public interest with rigorous legal structure.
tribune.associationsDesc: Champion collective causes with the community's support and vote.
tribune.fairSystem: A fair system: right of reply
tribune.fairSystemDesc: Every organization targeted by a citizen case has a right of reply published directly on the case page. Transparency for all sides.
tribune.fairPoint1: The targeted organization is notified of the case publication
tribune.fairPoint2: They can publish their official response, visible to all
tribune.fairPoint3: The community votes with full knowledge, seeing both sides
tribune.fairPoint4: No censorship: facts and responses are public
tribune.orgResponseText: We dispute the presented facts and provide the following clarifications...
tribune.ctaDesc: The People's Tribune is coming soon. Be among the first to build a citizen case.
tribune.alreadyRegistered: You are already registered. We will notify you!

about.heroDesc: We bring citizens together for collective legal actions. Together, we transform outrage into concrete legal proceedings.
about.fifthPowerDesc: In our democratic societies, there are traditionally three branches of power. Wejustice aims to create a fifth.
about.legislativeDesc: It passes the laws that govern our society.
about.executiveDesc: It enforces laws and governs daily affairs.
about.judiciaryDesc: It settles disputes and delivers Justice.
about.pressDesc: The 4th power: it informs and acts as a check on power.
about.citizensPowerDesc: The 5th power: together, we transform resistance into concrete means of action, activating and engaging Justice.
about.missionDesc: Three pillars guide each of our decisions.
about.pillarTransparencyDesc: Every citizen can follow actions and their progress.
about.pillarAccessibilityDesc: Taking legal action should not be reserved for those with the most resources. Through the power of numbers, our members act collectively in Justice.
about.pillarImpactDesc: We fight ambitious battles and we never give up.
about.timelineSubtitle: Key milestones in the rise of Wejustice
about.teamDesc: Justice should not be a privilege. To make it accessible, you need legal expertise and the ability to mobilize.
about.differentiatorDesc: Unlike old petition platforms, we don't stop at the signature: it's our starting point. Then, we take legal action.
about.classicPetitionsDesc: Symbolic signature collection with no legal follow-up. Impact relies solely on media pressure.
about.wejusticeDesc: Every signature feeds a real legal procedure, led by a specialized lawyer. Your rights are defended in court.
about.processDesc: From signatures to verdict, every step is transparent and collective.
about.ctaDesc: Join thousands of citizens who are collectively enforcing their rights.

actionDetail.milestoneGoal: Every signature brings us closer to the goal
actionDetail.milestoneGoalDesc: At each milestone reached, a new legal step is triggered.
actionDetail.journalDesc: Every step documented. Our actions and the adversary's responses.
actionDetail.followUpIntro: We report on every step. Here is what has been achieved thanks to your support and that of {count} signatories.

share.pageDesc: The more of us there are, the louder our voice. Share <<{title}>> with others.
